justification: All pass criteria met: both domain rules (state transition invariant and refund precondition) present with detailed testable_assertions; all three architecture decisions (OrderStateMachine, PATCH endpoint, RefundService) present with implementation_notes — exceeds the two required; acceptance_criteria preserves ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim in the SHIPPED→PENDING criterion; terminology_alignment is present and thoroughly addresses Bird/MJ language differences across three term pairs; task_context is one specific, actionable paragraph; no invented information detected — all details trace back to the source summaries.

observations.text: Three architecture decisions instead of the required two — RefundService separation is called out with the important detail about not reading state directly from the database
terminology_alignment goes beyond the minimum by mapping three concept pairs (state transition, invariant, issued) rather than just noting the SHIPPED/PATCH language difference
ORDER_STATE_INVALID preserved verbatim in acceptance_criteria with correct HTTP 422 status code
open_questions are well-formed and actionable — they flag genuine ambiguities (inter-process vs in-process, full transition matrix) without inventing answers
acceptance_criteria use precise Given/When/Then format with three distinct scenarios covering both invariants plus the happy path

terminology_alignment does not explicitly address the specific Bird 'SHIPPED' vs MJ 'PATCH /orders/{id}/state' language difference called out in the rubric — the alignment entries are more general concept mappings rather than reconciling that Bird says 'order status' and MJ says 'state'
